steps.title: Identify each text’s critique of Tuchman
steps.content: Text 1 argues that Tuchman failed to address Eastern European events before the war, which caused her to overstate Germany’s role as the war’s instigator — a flaw in the scope of her research leading to a wrong interpretation. Text 2 argues that her main thesis — that powers locked themselves into catastrophe by refusing to deviate from pre-war military plans — is “implausibly reductive,” meaning overly simplistic.

steps.title: Match each critique to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Text 1: Tuchman should have relied more on other historians; Text 2: her most interesting claims result from original research’: Text 1’s summary is accurate, but Text 2 never says her most interesting claims come from original research. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Text 1: the scope of Tuchman’s research led her to an incorrect interpretation; Text 2: Tuchman’s central argument is overly simplistic’: both halves match the texts exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Text 1: the writing style makes it worthwhile despite research flaws; Text 2: focuses exclusively on the weakness of interpretation’: Text 1 never says the book is worthwhile to read despite its flaws, and Text 2 actually praises Tuchman’s analysis of primary sources. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Text 1: Tuchman would agree WWI was largely due to Eastern Europe; Text 2: Eastern European leaders were not committed to military plans’: neither text makes these claims. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
